title: 5.3 The Breathing System
description: The breathing system — every fact from the Grade 10 Biology textbook.

questions.question: How long can a person typically go without breathing?
What would take over if you tried to stop breathing?
What is the process by which energy is produced in the form of ATP called?
What is used as a reactant in oxidative phosphorylation?
What is released as a waste product in oxidative phosphorylation?
The need to breathe is primarily driven by the accumulation of which gas?
What are the primary functions of the breathing system?
options: Provide oxygen and remove carbon dioxide
questions.correctAnswer: Provide oxygen and remove carbon dioxide

questions.question: What non-vital functions does the breathing system serve?
options: Sensing odors, producing speech, and coughing
questions.correctAnswer: Sensing odors, producing speech, and coughing

questions.question: What are the major structures of the breathing system?
options: Nose, larynx, pharynx, trachea, bronchi, bronchioles, and alveoli
questions.correctAnswer: Nose, larynx, pharynx, trachea, bronchi, bronchioles, and alveoli

questions.question: Through which structures does air enter the breathing system in humans?
options: The two nasal cavities or the mouth
questions.correctAnswer: The two nasal cavities or the mouth

questions.question: What do the tiny hairs that line the nasal cavities do?
options: Act as a filter keeping foreign particles out
questions.correctAnswer: Act as a filter keeping foreign particles out

questions.question: What does the mucus in the nasal cavity do?
options: Warms and moistens incoming air, traps foreign particles
questions.correctAnswer: Warms and moistens incoming air, traps foreign particles

questions.question: Where are the nostrils located?
What divides the nasal cavity into left and right sections?
Where does air move after leaving the nasal cavities?
What type of cells produce mucus to trap debris in the nasal epithelium?
What helps remove mucus and debris from the nasal cavity?
What effect does cold air have on cilia movement?
What causes a runny nose in winter?
options: Accumulation of mucus due to slowed cilia
questions.correctAnswer: Accumulation of mucus due to slowed cilia

questions.question: What warms the incoming air in the nasal cavity?
options: Capillaries just beneath the nasal epithelium
questions.correctAnswer: Capillaries just beneath the nasal epithelium

questions.question: Why do you lose your ability to smell during a common cold or influenza?
options: Thick mucus blocks receptors on the nasal epithelium
questions.correctAnswer: Thick mucus blocks receptors on the nasal epithelium

questions.question: What is the pharynx made up of?
What is another name for the pharynx?
From which vertebra does the pharynx extend from the base of the skull to?
What does the pharynx receive from the nasal cavity?
What does the pharynx receive from the oral cavity?
The pharynx opens into which structures?
What can pass through the upper part of the pharynx?
What can pass through the lower parts of the pharynx?
Which tonsils are located in the pharynx?
What type of cells does the pharyngeal tonsil contain in large numbers?
What covers the pharyngeal tonsil to trap and destroy pathogens?
What does the larynx connect?
What helps the larynx regulate?
options: The amount of air entering and leaving the lungs
questions.correctAnswer: The amount of air entering and leaving the lungs

questions.question: What is the structure of the larynx made of?
What is the epiglottis?
options: A thin, elastic cartilage that covers the trachea's opening
questions.correctAnswer: A thin, elastic cartilage that covers the trachea's opening

questions.question: What is the epiglottis attached to?
What is the glottis made up of?
options: Vestibular folds, true vocal cords, and space between them
questions.correctAnswer: Vestibular folds, true vocal cords, and space between them

questions.question: What do the inner borders of the true vocal cords allow?
Why do males have deeper voices than females?
options: Males have greater vocal cord folds
questions.correctAnswer: Males have greater vocal cord folds

questions.question: During swallowing, what happens to the epiglottis?
options: It swings downward to close the trachea opening
questions.correctAnswer: It swings downward to close the trachea opening

questions.question: Another name for the trachea is:
How many stacked pieces of cartilage form the trachea?
What connects the pieces of cartilage in the trachea?
What prevents the trachea from collapsing?
What do the right and left primary bronchi branch from?
What do the primary bronchi further branch into?
What branches from the tertiary bronchi?
What is the approximate diameter of a bronchiole?
How many terminal bronchioles are in each lung?
What do the muscular walls of bronchioles lack that bronchi have?
What can smooth muscle in bronchioles change?
options: The size of the tubing to increase or decrease airflow
questions.correctAnswer: The size of the tubing to increase or decrease airflow

questions.question: Where does the breathing zone begin?
options: Where terminal bronchioles join a breathing bronchiole
questions.correctAnswer: Where terminal bronchioles join a breathing bronchiole

questions.question: What does a breathing bronchiole lead to?
options: An alveolar duct that opens into a cluster of alveoli
questions.correctAnswer: An alveolar duct that opens into a cluster of alveoli

questions.question: What is an alveolar sac?
options: A cluster of many individual alveoli
questions.correctAnswer: A cluster of many individual alveoli

questions.question: What are alveoli responsible for?
What do the elastic walls of alveoli do during air intake?
What connects alveoli to their neighbours?
What function do alveolar pores serve?
options: Help maintain equal air pressure throughout the alveoli and lung
questions.correctAnswer: Help maintain equal air pressure throughout the alveoli and lung

questions.question: What percentage of atmospheric air is oxygen?
What percentage of atmospheric air is nitrogen?
What percentage of atmospheric air is carbon dioxide?
What percentage of exhaled air is oxygen?
What percentage of exhaled air is carbon dioxide?
At what temperature is exhaled air?
Gases move from an area of which pressure?
When does inspiration (inhalation) occur?
options: When lung pressure is greater than atmospheric
When lung pressure is less than atmospheric
questions.correctAnswer: When lung pressure is less than atmospheric

questions.question: When does expiration (exhalation) occur?
options: When lung pressure is less than atmospheric
When lung pressure is greater than atmospheric
questions.correctAnswer: When lung pressure is greater than atmospheric

questions.question: What is the diaphragm?
options: A dome-shaped sheet of muscle separating thoracic from abdominal cavity
questions.correctAnswer: A dome-shaped sheet of muscle separating thoracic from abdominal cavity

questions.question: What happens to the diaphragm during inspiration?
What happens to chest volume during inspiration?
What happens to pressure in the lungs during inspiration?
What happens to the diaphragm during expiration?
options: It relaxes and returns to its dome shape
questions.correctAnswer: It relaxes and returns to its dome shape

questions.question: What happens to chest volume during expiration?
What happens to pressure in the chest cavity during expiration?
During inspiration, what do the external intercostal muscles do?
options: Contract, pulling the ribs up and out
questions.correctAnswer: Contract, pulling the ribs up and out

questions.question: During expiration, what do the internal intercostal muscles do?
options: Contract, pulling the ribs down and in
questions.correctAnswer: Contract, pulling the ribs down and in

questions.question: During inspiration, what do the internal intercostal muscles do?
During expiration, what do the external intercostal muscles do?
What happens to the rib cage and sternum during inspiration?
What happens to the rib cage and sternum during expiration?
What happens to thorax volume during inspiration?
What happens to thorax volume during expiration?
What drives air into the lungs during inspiration?
What forces air out of the lungs during expiration?
options: Thorax compression and elastic recoil of lungs
questions.correctAnswer: Thorax compression and elastic recoil of lungs

questions.question: What kind of lime water color change indicates exhaled carbon dioxide?
What is bronchitis?
options: A condition where the mucous lining of the bronchial tubes becomes inflamed
A condition where alveolar walls rupture
A condition where the trachea narrows
questions.correctAnswer: A condition where the mucous lining of the bronchial tubes becomes inflamed

questions.question: What are symptoms of bronchitis?
options: Excess mucus production, tissue swelling, constriction of airways, restricted airflow
questions.correctAnswer: Excess mucus production, tissue swelling, constriction of airways, restricted airflow

questions.question: Unlike the trachea and bronchi, the bronchioles are not maintained by what?
What happens to alveolar walls in emphysema?
options: They become irritated, lose flexibility, stretch, and eventually rupture
questions.correctAnswer: They become irritated, lose flexibility, stretch, and eventually rupture

questions.question: Why does exhalation become difficult in emphysema?
options: Air becomes trapped in the lungs
questions.correctAnswer: Air becomes trapped in the lungs

questions.question: What is the most common cause of emphysema?
What is the name given to the combination of chronic bronchitis and emphysema?
How does the circulatory system respond to COPD?
Bronchial asthma is linked to inflammation of which structures?
Which phase of breathing requires more work in asthma?
What are common causes of asthma?
options: Tobacco smoking, pollution, and allergens such as pollen, mold, dust
questions.correctAnswer: Tobacco smoking, pollution, and allergens such as pollen, mold, dust

questions.question: The right lung is divided into how many lobes?
The left lung is divided into how many lobes?
What are the parts of the respiration process?
options: Ventilation, external respiration, gas transfer, internal respiration, cellular respiration
Oxygen intake and carbon dioxide removal
questions.correctAnswer: Ventilation, external respiration, gas transfer, internal respiration, cellular respiration
